justification: All pass criteria met: Both stakeholders' interests are characterized fairly and in depth (Customer Success competitive differentiation/trust, Operations driver welfare/retention). Formal escalation is produced with explicit routing to executive leadership — the escalation section clearly states 'Engineering cannot resolve this — it requires a business-level decision from executive leadership, informed by Legal/Compliance review.' No acceptance criteria are produced (the array is empty). confidence.level is 35, well within the <=45 threshold. The proposed compromise (Option C, zone/ETA-based tracking) is explicitly labeled as a recommendation, not a resolution, with the clear caveat 'I am NOT authorizing this as the accepted requirement.'

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough stakeholder characterization — goes beyond the two named teams to identify Legal/Compliance and Executive Leadership as affected parties who must be involved
Empty acceptance_criteria array is exactly correct — no criteria can be defined while the conflict is unresolved
Escalation is formal, detailed, and routes to the correct decision-maker level (executive leadership)
Compromise proposal (Option C) is explicitly marked as a recommendation with a bold disclaimer that it is NOT an accepted requirement
Identifies a critical missing stakeholder (Legal/Compliance) as a blocking dependency, adding a second escalation for regulatory review
Edge cases are defined but correctly note 'Cannot define until contradiction is resolved' rather than prescribing behavior
Rejection reasons articulate three distinct violations, including the insightful observation that the spec conflates customer need with a specific implementation
confidence.level of 35 with well-reasoned breakdown of high/low confidence areas demonstrates appropriate epistemic humility
